Class MatchSession

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am22.controller.server.MatchSession
public final class MatchSession extends Object
Per-match state: MatchController + VirtualView, isolated from other matches. Driven by MatchManager, which serializes all mutations via synchronized(session) before invoking the handle* methods.

Dependencies (mapper, persistence, DAO, end-game closer) are injected by the manager. Auto-removal from the manager's registry happens via the removeFromRegistry callback, so this class never references the manager directly.

    Builds a session around a controller restored from a disk snapshot.

  • Constructor Details

    • MatchSession

      public MatchSession(String matchId, ModelDtoMapper mapper, it.polimi.ingsw.am22.controller.server.persistence.MatchPersistence persistence, it.polimi.ingsw.am22.controller.server.datebases.MatchResultDao matchResultDao, ScheduledExecutorService endGameCloser, long endGameCloseDelayMs, Runnable removeFromRegistry)
      Builds a session for a brand-new (lobby-phase) match.
      Parameters:
      matchId - id of the new match
      mapper - model-to-DTO mapper shared by the manager
      persistence - disk store used for crash recovery
      matchResultDao - DAO for the historical leaderboard
      endGameCloser - scheduler used to defer the post-game channel close
      endGameCloseDelayMs - delay before closing channels after the end-game broadcast
      removeFromRegistry - callback that removes this session from the manager

    • MatchSession

      public MatchSession(MatchController restoredController, ModelDtoMapper mapper, it.polimi.ingsw.am22.controller.server.persistence.MatchPersistence persistence, it.polimi.ingsw.am22.controller.server.datebases.MatchResultDao matchResultDao, ScheduledExecutorService endGameCloser, long endGameCloseDelayMs, Runnable removeFromRegistry)
      Builds a session around a controller restored from a disk snapshot.

  • Method Details

    • getMatchController

      public MatchController getMatchController()
      Returns:
      the controller backing this match

    • snapshotChannels

      public Collection<ClientHandler> snapshotChannels()
      Snapshot of all currently bound client handlers — used by the RMI liveness probe.

    • isRecovering

      public boolean isRecovering()
      Returns:
      whether the match is paused waiting for players to reconnect after a crash

    • reconnectedCount

      public int reconnectedCount()
      Returns:
      how many players have reconnected so far during recovery

    • persistIfRunning

      public void persistIfRunning()
      Writes a snapshot of this match to disk if it is started and not over.

    • handleReconnect

      public void handleReconnect(ReconnectRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Resumes a suspended match for a reconnecting player. The match must be a started game and the nickname must belong to one of its players, otherwise an exception bubbles up as an ErrorMessage — this is the "nickname does not match" error the client surfaces.

    • handleAbandonRecovered

      public void handleAbandonRecovered(ClientHandler channel)
      Tears down a suspended match because a reconnecting player chose "Leave this match" instead of resuming it. The abandoning channel is not bound here (it reconnected only to send this request), so detaching it is a harmless no-op.

    • handleRecoveryTimeout

      public void handleRecoveryTimeout()
      Tears down a suspended match that nobody finished resuming within the allowed window. Called by the manager's recovery-timeout scheduler while the match is still recovering.

    • handleAddPlayer

      public void handleAddPlayer(AddPlayerToLobbyRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Handles a lobby-join request (routed to a reconnect if the match is crash-recovered).
      Parameters:
      request - the join request
      channel - the requesting channel

    • handleHostCreateAndSetup

      public void handleHostCreateAndSetup(String hostNickname, int expectedPlayers, ClientHandler channel)
      Coalesces "host joins + expected players set" into a single broadcast. Without this, the client would receive two LobbyStateMessages back-to-back (expected=0 then the requested value) and render the lobby twice.

    • handleSetExpectedPlayers

      public void handleSetExpectedPlayers(SetExpectedPlayersRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Handles the host's request to change the expected player count.
      Parameters:
      request - the request
      channel - the requesting channel

    • handleRemoveFromLobby

      public void handleRemoveFromLobby(RemovePlayerFromLobbyRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Handles a player voluntarily leaving the lobby.
      Parameters:
      request - the request
      channel - the requesting channel

    • handleChooseTotem

      public void handleChooseTotem(ChooseTotemRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Pre-game totem pick. Not a game move (no Game exists yet): it either advances the selection — broadcasting an updated TotemSelectionMessage — or, when the last player chooses, starts the game inside the controller, which publishStateChange(boolean) then surfaces as a GameStartedMessage.

    • handlePlaceTotem

      public void handlePlaceTotem(PlaceTotemRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      All three move handlers wrap the controller call in a VirtualView batch: a single move typically triggers 5-6 model notifications (state, active player, era, scoring...) and we want to coalesce them into one broadcast. The try/finally keeps the batch from staying open on exception.

    • handlePickCards

      public void handlePickCards(PickCardsRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Handles a card-pick move.
      Parameters:
      request - the request
      channel - the requesting channel

    • handlePickBonusCard

      public void handlePickBonusCard(PickBonusCardRequest request, ClientHandler channel)
      Handles an end-of-round bonus-card pick.
      Parameters:
      request - the request
      channel - the requesting channel

    • handleDisconnect

      public void handleDisconnect(String nickname, ClientHandler channel, boolean transportDrop)
      Handles both a voluntary leave (transportDrop=false) and a transport drop (transportDrop=true). Pre-game: the player just leaves the lobby. Mid-game: the match is aborted for everyone; surviving channels stay connected to the server (unbound) so they can list/create/join again.
